title: Mid-Semester Department Meeting Held!
content:
The Department of Economics and Finance held its mid-semester departmental meeting to review ongoing academic and administrative processes and to coordinate priorities for the remainder of the semester. During the meeting, emphasis was placed on the timely completion of course information packages within the student information system (OBS), with a structured follow-up process established to ensure completion within the designated timeframe. In addition, discussions were held regarding the revision and expansion of the Department Advisory Board and the external stakeholder list, with a view to strengthening institutional engagement and representation. The meeting also included an exchange of ideas on organizing academic events, particularly focusing on a potential symposium and a panel. It was decided to initiate preparations for a symposium to be held in the following Spring semester, while organizing a panel event within the current term. Furthermore, coordination processes related to data integration into departmental systems were addressed, with academic staff contributing to the transfer of relevant information into the Proliz system. The completion and standardization of course syllabus were also discussed, with an emphasis on timely submission and academic consistency across courses. The meeting concluded with decisions aimed at ensuring the timely completion of academic documentation, strengthening departmental coordination, and supporting the effective continuation of academic and administrative processes.

This content was updated on 10/04/2026.
